_comment: Pipeline-specific autonomous-mode settings merged with user's existing settings at runtime by `factory autonomy ensure`. User settings are the merge base; this template overlays permissions/env/statusLine/hooks.
_security_model: Bash(*) in permissions.allow is intentional in autonomous mode: no human-in-loop to approve granular Bash(...) prompts and the LLM cannot enumerate every command up front. The real security boundary is the plugin's checked-in, path-resolving hook dispatcher (dist/factory-hook.js, wired in hooks/hooks.json: branch-protection, secret-guard, pipeline-guards, holdout-guard, write-protection, stop-gate, subagent-stop) -- those hooks enforce factory correctness invariants and are complete for what they guard. permissions.deny below is deliberately short: a string-glob deny-list can be neither complete (misses ../.. climbing, absolute-path forms, nested shells) nor precise (can't tell a prod DB DROP from a test one), so against a non-adversarial coding agent it is scoped to accident-prevention for a handful of irreversible, non-file-path-shaped operations (interpreter -e/-c eval, npm/gh publish and remote-delete) plus paths that sit outside any task worktree and so aren't reachable by the hook layer at all (home credentials/config, the worktree's own .git/). The relaunch command (factory autonomy ensure) appends --permission-mode bypassPermissions, which suppresses Claude Code's built-in protected-path PROMPT for writes under this plugin's data dir (it lives under ~/.claude/, outside the `.claude/worktrees` exemption) -- permissions.deny, every hook, and the 'rm -rf /' / 'rm -rf ~' circuit-breaker still apply under bypass. Real containment against an adversarial agent is an OS sandbox (deferred, separate initiative), not a bigger deny-list. See docs/explanation/decisions.md Decision 65.

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); FP=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // .tool_input.pattern // empty'); [ -z "$FP" ] && exit 0; printf '%s' "$FP" | grep -qE '(^|/)\.claude(/|$)' || exit 0; printf '%s' "$FP" | grep -qE '(^|/)\.claude/worktrees/' && exit 0; PD='${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}'; PDT='${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA_TILDE}'; if [ -z "$PD" ] || [ -z "$PDT" ]; then echo '{"decision":"block","reason":"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA placeholders not substituted in merged-settings.json — relaunch the session."}'; exit 0; fi; case "$FP" in "$PD"/*|"$PDT"/*) exit 0 ;; esac; printf '%s' "$FP" | grep -qE '(^|/)\.claude/projects/[^/]+/[^/]+/tool-results/' && exit 0; echo '{"decision":"block","reason":"Access to .claude/ directory is restricted in autonomous mode."}'
FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path'); npx prettier --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; echo '{"suppressOutput": true}'

if [ -z "${FACTORY_TASK_ID:-}" ]; then exit 0; fi; FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '\.(test|spec|d)\.(ts|tsx)$|\.config\.(ts|tsx)$' && exit 0; echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$' || exit 0; { [ -f node_modules/.bin/vitest ] || jq -e '.devDependencies.vitest // .dependencies.vitest' package.json >/dev/null 2>&1; } || exit 0; npx vitest run --reporter=dot --findRelatedTests "$FILE" --passWithNoTests 2>&1 | tail -10 >&2; echo '{"suppressOutput": true}'

AUDIT=.claude/tool-audit.jsonl; if [ -f "$AUDIT" ] && [ $(stat -f%z "$AUDIT" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$AUDIT" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) -gt 10485760 ]; then mv "$AUDIT" "$AUDIT.old"; fi; jq -c --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" --arg task "${FACTORY_TASK_ID:-interactive}" '{ts: $ts, task: $task, tool: .tool_name, target: (.tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.command // null)}' >> "$AUDIT" 2>/dev/null || true
